user_query:
I want you to use a structured Plan → Build → Verify → Learn workflow to deliver a small, production-quality CLI utility and its documentation. Please read all the inputs under input/ first, then create everything only under output/.

Inputs to read:
- input/spec.md (utility requirements and API signatures)
- input/data.csv (sample data to convert)
- input/bug_report.txt (prior failure details to log and fix)

Deliverables (write exactly to these paths):
1) Planning & logs
- output/tasks/todo.md — a detailed plan with checkable items ("- [ ]"/"- [x]") covering: planning, implementation, tests, "Verification Before Done" steps, a brief "Demand Elegance" reflection, and any "Subagent/parallel work" notes you would use. Include a short results/review section at the end.
- output/tasks/lessons.md — capture at least one lesson using a structured entry with an ID like [LRN-YYYYMMDD-001] and metadata fields: Priority, Status, Area, Pattern-Key, Recurrence-Count, Source, Tags.
- output/tasks/errors.md — log the prior failure from input/bug_report.txt as an entry like [ERR-YYYYMMDD-001] with: Error Output, Context, Suggested Fix, Repro Steps, and Severity.
- output/tasks/feature_requests.md — add at least one [FEAT-YYYYMMDD-001] entry with: Capability name, Complexity Estimate, Suggested Implementation.
- output/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md — a brief session log for today with what you planned, built, verified, and learned.

2) Code & tests
- Implement the CLI utility at output/tools/csv_to_json.py exactly following the function and CLI requirements in input/spec.md. It must:
  - Expose transform_csv_to_json(csv_text: str) -> list[dict] and load_csv_and_write_json(in_path: str, out_path: str) -> None.
  - Support CLI usage: python output/tools/csv_to_json.py input.csv output.json
  - Meet the behavioral spec: first row = headers, ignore blank/whitespace-only lines, trim cell whitespace, parse numeric-looking values to numbers (int/float), case-insensitive "null" to null, and keep missing cells as null.
- Provide a self-contained test runner at output/tests/test_csv_to_json.py that can be executed with: python output/tests/test_csv_to_json.py and exits 0 on success. Include tests that validate: blank-line handling, whitespace trimming, numeric conversion, and "null" mapping.

3) Artifact
- Run your utility on input/data.csv and write the result to output/artifacts/data.json.

Constraints
- Use only the Python standard library.
- Make the plan and lessons good enough that a senior engineer would accept them.
- Do not write anywhere outside output/.

When finished, ensure all files are present at the paths above and that the test script passes locally. If you discover edge cases during verification, update output/tasks/lessons.md and output/tasks/errors.md accordingly.
